steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition for more than one real solution
steps.content:
For a quadratic equation to have more than one real solution, the discriminant must be positive.

In the equation $64x^2 + bx + 25 = 0$, we have $a = 64$ and $c = 25$. Using the discriminant condition:
$$b^2 - 4(64)(25) > 0$$
$$b^2 - 6400 > 0$$
$$b^2 > 6400$$

steps.title: Test which choice satisfies the condition
steps.content:
We need $b^2 > 6400$, which means $|b| > 80$.
  • Choice A: $b = -91$ gives $(-91)^2 = 8281 > 6400$ ✓
  • Choice B: $b = -80$ gives $(-80)^2 = 6400 ot> 6400$ ✗
  • Choice C: $b = 5$ gives $(5)^2 = 25 ot> 6400$ ✗
  • Choice D: $b = 40$ gives $(40)^2 = 1600 ot> 6400$ ✗
Only choice A satisfies the condition. That matches choice A — A is correct.
